title: Publisher Admission, Buffering, and Backpressure · Go Semantics Case: Blocked Unblocked Flow
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must bound in-flight confirms and bytes, consume blocked signals, serialize channel use, and propagate saturation upstream; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the blocked unblocked flow increment in a cancellable Go publisher admission controller. Learners progr…
objectives: Respond to blocked and unblocked connection events, broker flow control, alarms, and local drain evidence without confusing their scopes; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Batch by count, bytes, age, confirm window, and cancellation while retaining each message result and fairness; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
